signals.headline: April 2 Tariff Suspension Confirmed: 90-Day Pause on Major Increases, Retaining 10% Universal Tariff Amid Negotiations**
signals.raw:
April 2 Tariff Suspension Confirmed: 90-Day Pause on Major Increases, Retaining 10% Universal Tariff Amid Negotiations** | YES | HIGH confidence[3]

Federal Reserve Governor Waller confirmed in his April 14 speech that substantial portions of the April 2, 2026 tariff hikes—deemed "dramatically larger" than expected—were suspended last Wednesday (April 15) for 90 days to enable negotiations reducin

signals.headline: **Powell/Fed Context**: Powell (Dec 2025 remarks) flagged tariff-driven goods inflation peaking Q1 2026 (absent further
signals.raw: **Powell/Fed Context**: Powell (Dec 2025 remarks) flagged tariff-driven goods inflation peaking Q1 2026 (absent further rounds), with labor market weaker than data shows (payrolls overstating by ~60k/month); Fed "looking through" one-time effects as core non-tariff inflation nears 2%[2]. Waller's outlook aligns, noting Q1 GDP dip from import surge but strong jobs (228k in March)[3].

signals.headline: **Tariff Effects on GDP and Inflation**: Yale Budget Lab estimates current regime (post-suspension) shrinks long-run U
signals.raw: **Tariff Effects on GDP and Inflation**: Yale Budget Lab estimates current regime (post-suspension) shrinks long-run U.S. GDP by 0.1% ($27B annually), with 2026 growth temporarily boosted vs. 2025 but manufacturing gains offset by 2.0% construction contraction; Section 122's 10% tariffs expire in 150 days unless extended, dropping effective rates from 11.0% to 8.2%[1]. signals.headline: **Prediction Market Movers**: Suspension resolves immediate "shock" from April 2 announcement (which tanked markets[3]),
signals.raw: **Prediction Market Movers**: Suspension resolves immediate "shock" from April 2 announcement (which tanked markets[3]), but persistent uncertainty on permanence could shift Fed rate cut odds, inflation bets, and GDP forecasts; Goldman Sachs sees 1% inflation rise H2 2025-2026[4]. No newer April 16-22 confirmations in results.
